outcome: Made me feel closer to my classmate
notes: Paired-samples t test, POST - PRE, pairwise-complete. Effect size is the manuscript's average-SD d = mean_diff/((sd_pre+sd_post)/2) = 5.6446 vs reported 5.65 (absolute difference 0.0054). SPSS's difference-score d (dz) = 4.055 is NOT what the article reports. t = 25.322915 reproduces the reported t = 25.32 exactly. ALI (Phase 2): reproduced 'd(av) = 5.64, p < .001.' -> 'Not reproducible'. Ali used the same average-SD method, so his verdict on this result turns only on rounding at the second decimal of d, not on the test statistic. Validated across all 7 rows of Supplementary Table 1: all 7 t values reproduce exactly, d_av reproduces 3 of 7 published d values exactly (dz reproduces 1, the trivial 0.00), and no single formula reproduces the whole table. Raw .sav holds 77 rows; 39 have complete pre/post on this item.
